metadata.description: Houdini node chain training data for AI agents. Covers SOP, DOP, LOP, and CHOP networks with parameters, connections, and expected outputs.

terminology.node: A single operator in a network
terminology.context: The type of network — SOP (Surface OPs), DOP (Dynamics), LOP (USD/Solaris), CHOP (Channel), ROP (Render)
terminology.wire: A connection from the output of one node to the input of another
terminology.parm: A parameter on a node that controls its behavior
terminology.cook: When Houdini evaluates a node to produce output geometry/data
terminology.input: Numbered inputs on the left side of a node (0-indexed)
terminology.display_flag: The node whose output is rendered in the viewport
terminology.render_flag: The node used for final render output

chains.title: Basic procedural box
chains.goal: Create a simple box primitive at the origin
chains.output_description: A single unit cube centered at the world origin with 6 quad faces.

chains.title: Subdivided sphere with noise displacement
chains.goal: Create an organic lumpy sphere by subdividing and displacing with noise
chains.output_description: A high-resolution polygon sphere with lumpy noise displacement applied along normals.

chains.title: Scatter points on a surface and copy spheres
chains.goal: Scatter random points on a torus, then copy small spheres at each point
chains.output_description: 200 small spheres distributed randomly across the surface of a torus.

chains.title: Extrude polygon faces by attribute
chains.goal: Randomly extrude faces of a subdivided box using a random attribute

nodes.name: polyextrude1
nodes.note: Use the extrude_dist prim attribute to drive per-face extrusion. Use $EXTRUDE_DIST or ch() to reference the attribute in the dist field.

chains.output_description: A box with ~30% of its faces randomly extruded outward by varying distances, creating a spiky look.

chains.title: Boolean union of two shapes
chains.goal: Merge a sphere and a box using boolean union to create a compound shape
chains.output_description: A single clean mesh combining the sphere and the overlapping box into one unified shape.

chains.title: Procedural helix curve
chains.goal: Generate a helix curve using a wrangle and then sweep a circle along it

nodes.name: add1
nodes.note: Create a single seed point at origin

chains.output_description: A 3-turn helical tube with a circular cross-section, rising 3 units in height and 1 unit in radius.

chains.title: VEX color gradient by height (Y position)
chains.goal: Apply a red-to-blue color gradient based on each point's Y position using a VEX wrangle
chains.output_description: A sphere shaded red at the bottom, blending smoothly to blue at the top.

chains.title: L-System tree
chains.goal: Generate a branching tree structure using an L-System node
chains.output_description: A 2D-style organic branching tree generated by Prusinkiewicz's L-System rule.

chains.title: VDB from polygon mesh and smooth
chains.goal: Convert a polygon mesh to a VDB volume, smooth it, then convert back to mesh
chains.output_description: A smooth remeshed sphere. The VDB round-trip repairs the low-poly topology and produces a clean, even mesh.

chains.title: Voronoi fracture with connectivity
chains.goal: Fracture a box into Voronoi shards and separate them by connectivity
chains.output_description: 40 packed packed primitives representing Voronoi shards of the original box, ready for RBD simulation.

chains.title: Point cloud from geometry with velocity
chains.goal: Scatter points on a sphere, assign outward velocity attribute, visualize with trails
chains.output_description: 500 points on a sphere surface with randomized outward velocity vectors visualized as short trail lines.

chains.title: UV unwrap and texture projection
chains.goal: Create a UV layout on a torus using auto UV and visualize it
chains.output_description: A torus with unwrapped UVs, visualized with a 4x4 checker pattern to verify UV stretching.

chains.title: Procedural building facade
chains.goal: Create a grid of rectangular panels on a box face using polyextrude and copy stamping
chains.output_description: A building-like box with the front face divided into inset panel windows by per-face extrusion.

chains.title: Point cloud particle burst
chains.goal: Create an animated point burst using sin/cos in a wrangle driven by $T (time)

nodes.name: null1
nodes.note: OUT_BURST — label the final node for clarity

chains.output_description: 500 points exploding outward from the origin in random directions, animated over time using $T.

chains.title: Basic RBD rigid body simulation
chains.goal: Drop Voronoi-fractured pieces onto a static ground plane using RBD

sop_setup.description: Inside a geometry node, create fractured pieces (see sop_010) and a ground plane. Bring both into DOP via dopimport.

chains.output_description: Fractured pieces fall under gravity and come to rest on the ground plane with real-world physics.

chains.title: FLIP fluid simulation (water pouring)
chains.goal: Simulate a small volume of water pouring from a source into a container
chains.output_description: A FLIP fluid simulation where particles flow into a container. Render by converting particles to VDB surface.

chains.title: Pyro fire and smoke simulation
chains.goal: Emit fire and smoke from a sphere source using Pyro solver
chains.output_description: A pyro volume simulation with rising fire and smoke emitted from a sphere-shaped source.

chains.title: USD stage setup with sphere light and Karma render
chains.goal: Create a USD stage with a sphere primitive, a sphere light, and render with Karma
chains.output_description: A USD stage with a shaded sphere illuminated by a warm sphere light, rendered to EXR via Karma.

chains.title: Attribute transfer between meshes
chains.goal: Transfer Cd color from a dense sphere to a low-poly grid based on proximity
chains.output_description: A flat grid colored by proximity-sampling the colorful sphere — a gradient UV-like pattern projected from 3D.

chains.title: Soft body deformation using wire solver input
chains.goal: Create a worm-like mesh that deforms along a curve using wire deform

nodes.name: curve1_deformed
nodes.note: This is the target deformed spine curve

chains.output_description: A tube mesh deformed to follow a curved spine, bending like a worm or snake body.

chains.title: Measure and visualize curvature
chains.goal: Measure mean curvature on a bumpy sphere and visualize it as color
chains.output_description: A noisy sphere colored green where convex (positive curvature) and red where concave (negative curvature).

chains.title: Procedural city block grid
chains.goal: Generate a grid of city blocks with random building heights using copy stamping

nodes.name: attribwrangle2
nodes.note: Scale each building individually after copy. More reliable: use copytopoints with transform by attribute.

chains.output_description: A 6x6 grid of randomized box buildings with varying heights and footprint sizes, forming a stylized city block.

chains.title: Edge group selection and bevel
chains.goal: Select all hard edges on a box and bevel them for a smooth industrial look

nodes.name: groupexpression1
nodes.note: Select all edges (expression=1 means all)

chains.output_description: A box with all 12 edges chamfered into 3-segment rounded fillets, giving it a clean machined appearance.

chains.title: Vellum cloth simulation setup
chains.goal: Prepare a grid mesh for Vellum cloth simulation with constraints
chains.output_description: A cloth panel pinned at its left and right edges, drooping under gravity like a curtain hung between two poles.

chains.title: Crowd placement on terrain
chains.goal: Scatter agent placeholder points on a terrain mesh respecting slope angle
chains.output_description: 500 agent placement points distributed across flat areas of a procedural terrain, avoiding steep slopes.

chains.title: Common VEX snippets reference
chains.goal: Reference card for frequently used VEX patterns in Houdini wrangles

snippets.label: Remap attribute range
snippets.code: float val = fit(@myattr, srcmin, srcmax, dstmin, dstmax);

snippets.label: Soft selection falloff by distance
Read neighbor point positions (pcfind)
Drive transform by time (oscillation)
Turbulent noise displacement

chains.title: Procedural chain link geometry
chains.goal: Create a 3D chain of interlocked torus rings using copy-to-curve instancing

nodes.name: attribwrangle1

parms.snippet: if (@ptnum % 2 == 0) { matrix3 m = ident(); rotate(m, PI * 0.5, {0,1,0}); @orient = quaternion(m); }

nodes.note: Alternate every other ring 90 degrees so they interlock

chains.output_description: A chain of 9 torus rings along a vertical line, every alternating ring rotated 90° to simulate interlocking chain links.

chains.title: Signed distance field based selection
chains.goal: Use a VDB SDF to mask which points of a scattered set fall inside a volume
chains.output_description: Only points that fall inside the sphere volume remain; outer points are deleted using SDF sign testing.

chains.title: Animated growing vine on surface
chains.goal: Simulate a vine growing along a surface using a timeshift-driven carve on a curve

nodes.name: carve1
nodes.note: Animates the vine growing from frame 1 to 120

chains.output_description: A tubular vine that grows across the sphere surface along a shortest-path curve, animating from point to point over 120 frames.

chains.title: FLIP fluid with collision object
chains.goal: Set up a FLIP fluid sim with a sphere emitter and a static box collider
chains.output_description: A FLIP fluid emitted from a sphere, falling under gravity and colliding with a static box collision object.

chains.title: Pyro fire and smoke from object
chains.goal: Emit fire and smoke from an SOP geometry using the Pyro solver
chains.output_description: A Pyro sim emitting temperature and fuel from a sphere, producing rising fire and smoke driven by buoyancy.

chains.title: Constraint network for RBD glue
chains.goal: Simulate fractured geometry with glue constraints that break on impact
chains.output_description: Voronoi-fractured packed geo held together by glue constraints; pieces break apart when impact impulse exceeds the strength threshold.

chains.title: USD asset assembly with materials
chains.goal: Import SOP geometry, assign a USD preview material, and export to a USD file
chains.output_description: A USD stage with a single geo prim bound to a UsdPreviewSurface material, written to a binary USD file.

chains.title: Karma render setup with environment light
chains.goal: Set up a Karma CPU render in Solaris with a dome light, camera, and render settings
chains.output_description: A Karma CPU render outputting 1920x1080 EXR frames with HDRI dome lighting, a 50mm camera, and AI denoising enabled.

chains.title: USD point instancer for crowd
chains.goal: Use a USD PointInstancer prim to scatter thousands of instances efficiently
chains.output_description: A USD PointInstancer prim placing agent geometry at each crowd point position — renders thousands of characters with near-zero memory overhead.

chains.title: Audio-driven parameter animation
chains.goal: Read an audio file, extract amplitude, and use it to drive a geometry parameter

nodes.name: chopnet_out
nodes.note: Reference channel with ch('/obj/geo1/chopnet1/chopnet_out/bass_amplitude')

chains.output_description: A channel named 'bass_amplitude' that tracks the RMS energy of bass frequencies in an audio file — reference it in any parm with ch().

chains.title: Procedural camera shake
chains.goal: Generate procedural camera shake using noise and apply it to a camera transform

nodes.name: filter1
nodes.note: Smooth out high-frequency jitter for more natural motion

chains.output_description: All 6 DOF of a camera driven by Gaussian-filtered sparse noise, creating smooth organic camera shake.

chains.title: Spring constraint for secondary animation
chains.goal: Add spring follow and overshoot to an animated object's translation channel

nodes.name: object_chout1
nodes.note: Wire this into /obj/secondary_null CHOP constraint

chains.output_description: Position channels with spring follow behaviour — the secondary object lags and overshoots the primary driven object for juicy secondary animation.

chains.title: Mantra render with AOVs
chains.goal: Set up a Mantra ROP with multiple image planes (AOVs) for compositing
chains.output_description: A Mantra IFD render outputting multi-layer EXR with beauty, N, P, diffuse, and specular AOVs for deep compositing.

chains.title: Geometry cache export (bgeo sequence)
chains.goal: Write a simulation cache to disk as a bgeo.sc sequence for geometry import
chains.output_description: Writes frames 1–240 of a DOP import geometry to compressed bgeo.sc files, one per frame.

chains.title: Deadline render farm submission
chains.goal: Configure a Houdini ROP network for submission to a Deadline render farm
chains.output_description: Submits the Karma render of frames 1001–1100 to Deadline in chunks of 5 frames at priority 60.

chains.title: Advanced VEX snippets reference
chains.goal: Reference card for advanced VEX patterns: matrices, noise types, tet/prim ops

snippets.label: Rotate point around arbitrary axis
Curl noise for velocity field
Build orient quaternion from velocity
Pack/unpack float attribute from int bits
Voronoi cell coloring by cell ID
Project point onto plane
Smooth step ease between two values
Ray-cast to find intersection with another mesh
Create polyline between every pair of nearby points
Chladni pattern on a grid

chains.title: Vellum grain simulation (sand pile)
chains.goal: Pour a stream of Vellum sand grains into a bowl-shaped collision object

sop_setup.description: In a SOP network, create an emitter sphere (small, positioned above the bowl) and a static bowl mesh. Connect both to dopimport nodes or reference them via soppath.

chains.output_description: Thousands of small sand grains pile up inside the bowl under gravity, exhibiting realistic grain-on-grain and grain-on-surface friction.

chains.title: Vellum wire simulation (hanging rope)
chains.goal: Simulate a rope hanging between two pinned endpoints using Vellum wire constraints

sop_setup.description: Create a resampled line (20+ points) in SOPs with two point groups: 'pin_start' (pt 0) and 'pin_end' (last pt). Wire the SOP path into the Vellum solver.

chains.output_description: A rope that droops naturally under gravity between its two fixed endpoints, responding to secondary forces like wind or collisions.

chains.title: POP particle network with forces
chains.goal: Emit particles from a surface, apply turbulence, drag, and kill them past a life threshold

nodes.name: popnet1
nodes.note: Double-click to enter the POP network. The nodes below live inside popnet1.

chains.output_description: A continuous stream of short-lived particles emitted from a sphere surface, curling through turbulence and slowing via drag before dying after their lifetime.

chains.title: Whitewater (foam/spray) from FLIP surface
chains.goal: Generate whitewater foam and spray on top of a cached FLIP sim using the Whitewater solver

sop_setup.description: Cache a FLIP sim to disk first (rop_002 pattern). Point the whitewater source at the cached particle sequence.

chains.output_description: Foam and spray particles generated at high-curvature and high-velocity regions of the FLIP surface, driven by the Whitewater solver.

chains.title: SOP pyro emission attribute prep
chains.goal: Prepare a mesh as a Pyro emitter by baking temperature, fuel, and velocity attributes onto points

nodes.name: volumebound1
nodes.note: Optional: creates an initial volume boundary to pre-size the Pyro container

nodes.name: OUT_emitter
nodes.note: Reference this node in the DOP sourcevolume soppath

chains.output_description: An emitter sphere carrying temperature, fuel, density, and outward velocity attributes, ready to be sourced into a Pyro DOP via sourcevolume.

chains.title: Smoke trail from moving object (SOP to Pyro)
chains.goal: Build a time-delayed point trail behind a moving object to use as a Pyro smoke emitter
chains.output_description: A uniformly spaced point trail following a moving object with density and temperature falling off toward the tail — ideal for a lingering smoke or exhaust Pyro emission source.

chains.title: Hard-surface crease and SubDivide workflow
chains.goal: Model a chamfered mechanical part by creasing control edges before OpenSubdiv subdivision

nodes.name: groupexpression1
nodes.note: Select the support loop edges added by the inset

parms.snippet:
// Mark crease on selected edges via crease attribute
// Alternatively use the 'crease' SOP directly

chains.output_description: A smooth subdivided box that retains sharp, machined edges at the inset support loops — a classic hard-surface subdivision workflow.

chains.title: Skin node between profile curves
chains.goal: Build an organic hull by skinning across a series of cross-section NURBS curves
chains.output_description: A smooth vase-shaped NURBS surface created by skinning four circular cross-sections at different heights and radii.

chains.title: PolyBridge between two polygon loops
chains.goal: Bridge two circular holes in a mesh to create a handle or tunnel connection

nodes.name: groupexpression1
nodes.note: Select face groups to delete to create the two openings

nodes.name: groupexpression2
nodes.note: Group each border loop for the bridge. In practice use Select mode in viewport.

chains.output_description: A box with two holes bridged by a 4-division tunnel of quads — the classic technique for adding handles or through-holes in hard-surface models.

chains.title: Mirror and merge for symmetrical modelling
chains.goal: Model half of an object, mirror it, and fuse the seam for a symmetrical result

nodes.name: box1
nodes.note: Model only the X+ half. Center at [0.5,0,0] so left edge sits exactly on X=0 plane.

nodes.name: edit1
nodes.note: Hand-sculpt the half-model here using the Edit SOP in symmetry mode or freehand

chains.output_description: A symmetrical object built by mirroring one half across the X axis and fusing coincident seam points into clean shared vertices.

chains.title: Boolean subtract hard-surface panel cut
chains.goal: Cut a recessed panel into a flat surface using boolean subtraction of an inset box
chains.output_description: A flat panel slab with a rectangular recess cut into its surface — a standard kitbash panel detail achieved via boolean subtract and a minor face inset.

chains.title: Remesh and uniform topology cleanup
chains.goal: Remesh an irregular imported mesh to produce uniform triangle density suitable for sculpting or simulation
chains.output_description: An imported mesh resampled to nearly-uniform triangle density via a VDB round-trip, then Laplacian-smoothed — ideal as a base mesh for sculpting or cloth simulation.

chains.title: Multi-res subdivision modelling with PolyReduce
chains.goal: Build a high-res sculpt proxy from a low-poly cage and generate a game-res LOD

nodes.name: subdivide1
nodes.note: High-res version for sculpt/render

chains.output_description: A Switch node toggling between the full-subdivision high-res mesh and the PolyReduce game-LOD — a practical multi-res pipeline inside a single SOP network.

chains.title: Point Cloud Lookup (pcopen/pcfilter)
chains.goal: Use VEX to average point colors from nearby points in a point cloud lookup
chains.output_description: A sphere with randomized point colors that are smoothly blurred by averaging colors from the nearest 8 neighbors using point cloud functions.

chains.title: For-Each Loop (Block Modeling)
chains.goal: Iterate over pieces of a fractured object and apply individual transformations
chains.output_description: A box fractured into pieces, where each individual piece is extruded inward and outward independently within a loop.

chains.title: KineFX Character Setup
chains.goal: Load a character and apply a basic KineFX skeleton modification

nodes.name: rigpose1
nodes.note: Allows manual joint manipulation in the viewport

chains.output_description: A character mesh deformed by its skeleton using the KineFX Joint Deform node, with a Rig Pose node for posing.

chains.title: Instructions for AI agent generating Houdini node chains
chains.goal: Guide an AI agent to correctly author Houdini node chains from this dataset
rules: Always specify the 'context' field (SOP, DOP, LOP, CHOP, ROP) — wrong context = wrong node palette.
Node 'type' must exactly match Houdini's internal operator type string (e.g. 'attribwrangle' not 'vex wrangle').
Connections are directional: from_output (usually 0) feeds into to_input (0-indexed). Multi-input nodes like merge, boolean, copytopoints use to_input 0,1,2.
Only one node should have display_flag=true. It defines what shows in the viewport.
VEX snippets in attribwrangle use @ to read/write point/prim/detail attributes. Class matters: 'point' for @ptnum access, 'detail' for @opinput0 and global ops.
Use $T or $F in parameter fields (HScript). In VEX snippets, use @Time and @Frame for time and frame values.
Always include a parms object even if empty {}. Missing parms cause cook errors.
node 'position' is [column, row] in network editor grid units — use [0,0], [0,-1], [0,-2] for vertical chains.
Null nodes named OUT_SOMETHING are best practice to mark network outputs.
For DOPs: the solver node (rbdsolver, flipsolver, pyrosolver, vellumsolver) is always last in the chain.
For LOPs: end chains with a karma or usdrender node when rendering is the goal.
VDB workflows: vdbfrompolygons → vdb ops → convertvdb back to mesh.
Scatter+CopyToPoints is the standard pattern for instancing geometry at points.
When referencing other nodes in parameters, use channel reference syntax: ch('../nodename/parmname') or opstyle paths.
